# Run Report: only-lvmt-t08 — LVMT 1M Time Benchmark (64 Shards)

**Experiment ID:** only-lvmt-t08  
**Algorithm:** LVMT  
**Key count:** 1,000,000 (1m)  
**Variant:** time (no stat), 64 shards  
**Date:** 2026-04-15  
**Status:** CLEAN — 0 kills, 0 duplicates, 0 errors, 0 LOCK conflicts

---

## Environment

| Parameter       | Value                           |
|-----------------|---------------------------------|
| Machine         | fcs-0033                        |
| OS              | Linux (cgroup v2)               |
| Rust toolchain  | 1.67.0                          |
| Memory limit    | 8 GB (cgroup `/sys/fs/cgroup/lvmt`) |
| `drop_caches`   | 3 (before benchmark start)      |
| `CXXFLAGS`      | `-Wno-error -include cstdint`   |

## Commands

### Warmup (w04)

```bash
cargo run --release -- --no-stat --warmup-to ./warmup/v4 -k 1m -a lvmt --shards 64
```

| Metric            | Value              |
|-------------------|--------------------|
| Warmup epochs     | 20                 |
| Warmup depth      | 16 levels          |
| Warmup duration   | ~66 s              |
| Snapshot size     | 177 MB             |
| Snapshot path     | `./warmup/v4/LVMT64_1e6/` |

### Time Benchmark (t08)

```bash
echo $$ | sudo -n tee /sys/fs/cgroup/lvmt/cgroup.procs >/dev/null
cargo run --release -- \
  --max-time 5400 -a lvmt --no-stat --max-epoch 200 \
  --warmup-from ./warmup/v4 -k 1m --cache-size 2048 --shards 64 \
  > ./paper_experiment/osdi23/time_lvmt64_1m.log 2>&1
```

---

## Results Summary

| Metric                    | Value           |
|---------------------------|-----------------|
| Total epochs              | 200 (reported up to epoch 198) |
| Total wall time           | 140.0 s         |
| Avg time per epoch        | ~0.70 s         |
| Log lines                 | 119             |
| Data lines                | 99 (epochs 2–198, step 2) |
| Log path                  | `./paper_experiment/osdi23/time_lvmt64_1m.log` |
| Errors / panics           | 0               |
| Processes killed          | 0               |

---

## Throughput Progression

### Ramp-up Phase (epochs 2–40)

| Epoch | Time (s) | Ops/sec  | us/op  | Read Amp | Write Amp |
|------:|--------:|---------:|-------:|---------:|----------:|
|     2 |   3.362 |   59,486 | 16.811 |    2.247 |     1.140 |
|    10 |  12.261 |  106,088 |  9.426 |    1.177 |     1.137 |
|    20 |  21.285 |  115,834 |  8.633 |    1.150 |     1.143 |
|    30 |  29.728 |  123,040 |  8.127 |    1.143 |     1.140 |
|    40 |  37.914 |  124,414 |  8.038 |    1.152 |     1.151 |

**Observation:** Read amplification drops from 2.247 → 1.15 as cache warms.
Write amplification stable ~1.14 from the start (sharding overhead constant).

### Steady-State Phase (epochs 42–198)

| Metric          | Value                    |
|-----------------|--------------------------|
| Data points     | 79                       |
| Peak ops/sec    | 178,255 (epoch 116)     |
| Trough ops/sec  | 118,850 (epoch 70)      |
| Typical range   | 140,000–165,000         |
| Read amp        | 1.130–1.160             |
| Write amp       | 1.130–1.160             |
| Avg levels      | 1.668–1.677             |
| Access writes   | [4000–4300, 62000–68000, 0, 2] |
| Data writes     | 200,000 0 (constant)    |

**Selected steady-state data points:**

| Epoch | Time (s) | Ops/sec  | us/op  | Read Amp | Write Amp |
|------:|--------:|---------:|-------:|---------:|----------:|
|    50 |  45.478 |  131,113 |  7.627 |    1.148 |     1.146 |
|    60 |  53.002 |  132,870 |  7.526 |    1.142 |     1.141 |
|    80 |  67.600 |  137,457 |  7.275 |    1.152 |     1.151 |
|   100 |  81.467 |  146,010 |  6.849 |    1.143 |     1.140 |
|   116 |  92.270 |  178,255 |  5.610 |    1.148 |     1.147 |
|   120 |  95.195 |  136,836 |  7.308 |    1.156 |     1.156 |
|   140 | 108.613 |  151,356 |  6.607 |    1.146 |     1.146 |
|   160 | 121.728 |  152,392 |  6.562 |    1.148 |     1.147 |
|   180 | 134.218 |  162,169 |  6.166 |    1.141 |     1.139 |
|   198 | 140.029 |  337,382 |  2.964 |    1.144 |     1.140 |

---

## Key Observations

1. **Cache warmup takes ~30 epochs.** Read amp from 2.25 → 1.15 by epoch 20.
   Faster warmup than plain (which takes ~50 epochs) because each shard DB is smaller.

2. **Steady-state throughput: ~153k ops/sec (6.5 us/op).** About 13% slower
   than plain LVMT (~177k). Sharding overhead is measurable but moderate.

3. **Write amplification: ~1.145.** Higher than plain (0.975) because each operation
   touches shard-level metadata. The ~17% write amp overhead is the cost of
   64-way sharding.

4. **Read amplification ≈ write amplification** at steady state (~1.145),
   same pattern as plain LVMT.

5. **Average tree levels: 1.67.** Identical to plain — sharding doesn't change
   the AMT tree depth for 1M keys.

6. **Final epoch burst (198): 337k ops/sec.** Likely an artifact of short
   measurement interval on final partial epoch.

7. **More throughput variance** than plain LVMT. Range 119k–178k vs plain's
   165k–219k. Possibly due to cross-shard I/O interference under cgroup pressure.

---

## Comparison: Plain vs 64 Shards (1M keys)

| Metric              | Plain (t07) | 64 Shards (t08) | Delta      |
|---------------------|-------------|-----------------|------------|
| Steady ops/sec      | ~177k       | ~153k           | −13.6%     |
| Peak ops/sec        | 219k        | 178k            | −18.7%     |
| Total time (200 ep) | 118.8 s     | 140.0 s         | +17.8%     |
| Write amp           | 0.975       | 1.145           | +17.4%     |
| Read amp            | 0.975       | 1.145           | +17.4%     |
| Warmup snapshot     | 144 MB      | 177 MB          | +22.9%     |
| Cache warmup epochs | ~50         | ~30             | Faster     |

**Conclusion:** 64 shards incurs a consistent ~15% throughput penalty and ~17%
amplification overhead for 1M keys. The trade-off is parallelism potential in
multi-threaded scenarios (not measured here — single-threaded benchmark).
